query_normalized: llmxive follow-up: extending "evopolicygym: evaluating autonomous policy evolution in interactive en" computer science
rationale: This paper satisfies criteria (d) and (e) as it provides a canonical simulation platform (MACAD-Gym) and a specific multi-agent environment for evaluating autonomous policy evolution, which directly supports the "Interactive Environments" and "Evaluating" components of the user's question regarding EvoPolicyGym. It establishes the necessary empirical baseline and methodological framework for studying autonomous policy adaptation in complex, non-stationary settings.
